experience_band.ideal: 6-8 years total, 4-5 in applied ML/AI at product companies

required_skills: Production embeddings-based retrieval (sentence-transformers, BGE, E5, OpenAI embeddings, etc.)
Vector databases or hybrid search (Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus, OpenSearch, Elasticsearch, FAISS)
Strong Python with production code quality
Ranking evaluation frameworks (NDCG, MRR, MAP, offline-to-online correlation, A/B testing)

explicit_disqualifiers.rule: Career spent in academic/research-only roles without production deployment
AI experience primarily recent (<12 months) LangChain + OpenAI calls without pre-LLM production ML
Senior engineer who has not written production code in 18+ months (architecture/tech-lead only)
Entire career at consulting firms (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, Cognizant, Capgemini, etc.) with no product-company experience
Primary expertise in computer vision, speech, or robotics without significant NLP/IR exposure
5+ years only on closed proprietary systems with no external validation (papers, talks, OSS)
Frequent company switches every <1.5 years optimizing for title progression
Profile dominated by LangChain tutorials/demos rather than systems thinking

location.country_requirement: India preferred; outside India case-by-case, no visa sponsorship
location.work_mode: Hybrid flexible; quarterly offsite travel expected

up_weight_if: Active on platform / open to work

ideal_candidate_profile.must_have_shipped: End-to-end ranking, search, or recommendation system at meaningful scale
ideal_candidate_profile.must_demonstrate: Strong opinions on hybrid vs dense retrieval, offline vs online eval, fine-tune vs prompt — grounded in systems they built
ideal_candidate_profile.location: Noida or Pune, or willing to relocate
ideal_candidate_profile.availability: Active on Redrob / clear job-market signal

hackathon_trap_guidance.do_not: Rank by AI keyword count or embedding similarity alone
hackathon_trap_guidance.do: Reason about title vs skills credibility gap, career-history corroboration, assessment scores vs claimed proficiency, behavioral availability, honeypot timeline impossibilities
hackathon_trap_guidance.plain_language_tier: Candidates may describe recommendation/search/ranking work without buzzwords like RAG or Pinecone — career history matters more than skills list

scoring_context.expected_strong_matches_in_100k: Very few (~10 great matches per JD text)
